source_boundary.authoritative_source: Specsight documentation pages in the Working with the spec section
source_boundary.interpretation_rule: This taxonomy encodes documented product functions, capabilities, relationships, and boundaries. It does not include ICP assumptions, buying triggers, market pain points, workflow failure modes, or speculative value claims.

product_operating_model.primary_product_object: A code-derived, living product specification organised into features and plain-language behavioural scenarios.

features_and_scenarios.product_function: Organises a product specification into features and scenario-level descriptions of product behaviour using a Context, Action, and Outcome structure.
features_and_scenarios.boundary: Does not by itself prove that a scenario is an approved requirement, an executable automated test, or a substitute for strategy, design rationale, customer help content, or roadmap documentation.

ask_specsight.product_function: Provides a conversational interface for asking questions about the product specification and receiving answers grounded in features, scenarios, changelog entries, and digests.
ask_specsight.boundary: Does not provide authoritative business strategy, customer commitments, undocumented rationale, or external information unless that context exists inside Specsight.

annotations.product_function: Allows free-text notes to be attached to individual scenarios for questions, rationale, caveats, links, and supporting context that cannot be inferred directly from code.
annotations.boundary: Annotations are manually supplied and do not automatically validate whether the added context is current or correct.

changelog.product_function: Provides a chronological record of changes to a project's specification, including automatic syncs, manual edits, annotations, and releases with no relevant behavioural change.
changelog.boundary: Is not necessarily a complete engineering audit log, source-control replacement, incident timeline, or explanation of why every code change occurred.

feature_map.product_function: Provides a project-level visual model of product features and their relationships plus a feature-level view of the ordered flow within a selected feature.
feature_map.boundary: Is not automatically a complete technical architecture diagram, service dependency map, data lineage model, or guaranteed impact analysis.

digests.product_function: Summarises how a project's specification changed across a selected date range and can be generated on demand or on a schedule.
digests.boundary: Is not automatically a marketing release note, legal customer notice, roadmap status report, incident report, or adoption-impact analysis.

automatic_syncing.product_function: Watches a selected branch and automatically analyses relevant release changes to update affected scenarios in the product specification.
automatic_syncing.boundary: Does not prove runtime correctness, production adoption, customer value, performance, test coverage, or conformity with original product intent.

export_the_spec.product_function: Allows the full specification, an individual feature, and digests to be exported for use outside Specsight in supported formats including PDF, XLSX, and Markdown.
export_the_spec.boundary: An export is a point-in-time copy and does not remain current after later releases unless exported again.

behaviour_level_product_representation.name: Behaviour-level product representation
behaviour_level_product_representation.definition: Represents the product in terms of externally meaningful behaviour rather than implementation details alone.

feature_based_product_organisation.name: Feature-based product organisation
feature_based_product_organisation.definition: Groups related behaviour into meaningful product or user-facing feature areas.

scenario_level_behavioural_decomposition.name: Scenario-level behavioural decomposition
scenario_level_behavioural_decomposition.definition: Breaks broad features into specific situations that can be read and reviewed independently.

context_action_outcome_clarity.name: Context-action-outcome clarity
context_action_outcome_clarity.definition: Uses a consistent structure to describe the conditions, interaction, and resulting behaviour of a scenario.

happy_path_edge_case_and_error_visibilit.name: Happy-path, edge-case, and error visibility
happy_path_edge_case_and_error_visibilit.definition: Makes normal flows, blocked actions, validation behaviour, edge cases, and error behaviour visible together.

plain_language_accessibility.name: Plain-language accessibility
plain_language_accessibility.definition: Makes product behaviour understandable to people who do not read source code.

human_review_and_correction.name: Human review and correction
human_review_and_correction.definition: Allows users to add, edit, or delete generated scenarios when clarification or correction is needed.

natural_language_product_inquiry.name: Natural-language product inquiry
natural_language_product_inquiry.definition: Allows users to ask questions about the product in ordinary language.

specification_grounded_answers.name: Specification-grounded answers
specification_grounded_answers.definition: Uses the product specification and its represented history as the basis for answers.

source_backed_product_explanations.name: Source-backed product explanations
source_backed_product_explanations.definition: Provides supporting sources that users can inspect behind an answer.

cross_feature_knowledge_access.name: Cross-feature knowledge access
cross_feature_knowledge_access.definition: Supports questions whose answers may span multiple features, scenarios, or historical changes.

historical_product_inquiry.name: Historical product inquiry
historical_product_inquiry.definition: Supports questions about how represented product behaviour changed over time.

self_service_product_knowledge_access.name: Self-service product knowledge access
self_service_product_knowledge_access.definition: Makes product information accessible without routing every question through an engineer or internal expert.

human_context_enrichment.name: Human context enrichment
human_context_enrichment.definition: Adds business or organisational context to a code-derived behavioural scenario.

scenario_level_collaboration.name: Scenario-level collaboration
scenario_level_collaboration.definition: Keeps discussion and clarification attached to the exact product behaviour being discussed.

decision_rationale_capture.name: Decision-rationale capture
decision_rationale_capture.definition: Preserves explanations for why a behaviour exists or why a decision was made.

caveat_and_constraint_documentation.name: Caveat and constraint documentation
caveat_and_constraint_documentation.definition: Records limitations, exceptions, dependencies, or operational considerations not apparent from implementation alone.

linked_evidence_attachment.name: Linked-evidence attachment
linked_evidence_attachment.definition: Associates related tickets or external references with a scenario.

shared_organisational_visibility.name: Shared organisational visibility
shared_organisational_visibility.definition: Makes added context visible across the organisation rather than leaving it in private conversations or individual memory.

product_behaviour_change_history.name: Product-behaviour change history
product_behaviour_change_history.definition: Records how represented product behaviour evolves over time.

release_to_specification_traceability.name: Release-to-specification traceability
release_to_specification_traceability.definition: Connects releases and sync activity to changes in the product specification.

added_changed_and_removed_behaviour_visi.name: Added, changed, and removed behaviour visibility
added_changed_and_removed_behaviour_visi.definition: Shows whether behavioural elements were introduced, modified, or removed.

human_versus_system_change_attribution.name: Human-versus-system change attribution
human_versus_system_change_attribution.definition: Distinguishes automatic product-analysis updates from manual human edits when attribution is available.

no_behaviour_change_confirmation.name: No-behaviour-change confirmation
no_behaviour_change_confirmation.definition: Records releases analysed without a resulting behavioural specification change.

feature_and_scenario_level_history.name: Feature- and scenario-level history
feature_and_scenario_level_history.definition: Allows inspection of the evolution of a selected feature or scenario.

historical_reconstruction.name: Historical reconstruction
historical_reconstruction.definition: Supports understanding what the product specification showed at different points in its evolution.

product_wide_structural_visibility.name: Product-wide structural visibility
product_wide_structural_visibility.definition: Shows product features as a connected system rather than an isolated list.

cross_feature_relationship_mapping.name: Cross-feature relationship mapping
cross_feature_relationship_mapping.definition: Represents how one feature leads to, authenticates, triggers, notifies, or otherwise connects with another feature.

dependency_awareness.name: Dependency awareness
dependency_awareness.definition: Helps users identify product areas that may rely on or interact with one another.

change_blast_radius_awareness.name: Change blast-radius awareness
change_blast_radius_awareness.definition: Supports inspection of connected feature areas that may warrant attention when one part of the product changes.

feature_level_flow_visualisation.name: Feature-level flow visualisation
feature_level_flow_visualisation.definition: Shows the ordered internal behavioural flow of an individual feature.

user_journey_and_system_flow_comprehensi.name: User-journey and system-flow comprehension
user_journey_and_system_flow_comprehensi.definition: Makes multi-step product paths easier to understand through a visual model.

time_bounded_change_summarisation.name: Time-bounded change summarisation
time_bounded_change_summarisation.definition: Summarises represented product-behaviour changes occurring during a chosen period.

on_demand_stakeholder_reporting.name: On-demand stakeholder reporting
on_demand_stakeholder_reporting.definition: Generates a current change summary when a specific communication need arises.

scheduled_product_change_reporting.name: Scheduled product-change reporting
scheduled_product_change_reporting.definition: Supports recurring product-change summaries without manual assembly each time.

change_synthesis.name: Change synthesis
change_synthesis.definition: Consolidates multiple scenario- and feature-level changes into a more consumable report.

behaviour_change_communication.name: Behaviour-change communication
behaviour_change_communication.definition: Organises updates around added, changed, and removed product behaviour rather than raw engineering activity.

shareable_product_updates.name: Shareable product updates
shareable_product_updates.definition: Produces product-change information designed to be forwarded to stakeholders with less rewriting.

release_triggered_specification_maintena.name: Release-triggered specification maintenance
release_triggered_specification_maintena.definition: Updates the product specification when releases are made to the watched branch.

incremental_behavioural_reanalysis.name: Incremental behavioural re-analysis
incremental_behavioural_reanalysis.definition: Focuses subsequent analysis on affected behaviour rather than repeatedly analysing the complete product.

code_to_specification_alignment.name: Code-to-specification alignment
code_to_specification_alignment.definition: Uses changes in implementation to keep the behavioural specification current.

documentation_drift_reduction.name: Documentation-drift reduction
documentation_drift_reduction.definition: Reduces reliance on people remembering to update a separate product-behaviour document after every release.

continuous_product_behaviour_visibility.name: Continuous product-behaviour visibility
continuous_product_behaviour_visibility.definition: Maintains an evolving view of represented product behaviour across successive releases.

change_history_accumulation.name: Change-history accumulation
change_history_accumulation.definition: Builds a historical record of product evolution through repeated release syncs.

portable_product_specification.name: Portable product specification
portable_product_specification.definition: Makes specification content usable outside the Specsight interface.

full_specification_export.name: Full-specification export
full_specification_export.definition: Allows the complete project specification to be downloaded as an external artifact.

feature_specific_export.name: Feature-specific export
feature_specific_export.definition: Allows one selected product area to be shared or reviewed without exporting the entire project.

multi_format_distribution.name: Multi-format distribution
multi_format_distribution.definition: Supports different stakeholder and workflow needs through PDF, XLSX, and Markdown formats.

offline_review.name: Offline review
offline_review.definition: Enables product behaviour to be reviewed without live access to the Specsight application.

existing_workflow_interoperability.name: Existing-workflow interoperability
existing_workflow_interoperability.definition: Allows specification content to be used in external documentation, review, audit, handoff, and communication workflows.
